description.en: When enabled, this feature logs packets with un\-routable source addresses to the kernel log.

rationale.en: Setting `net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians` and `net.ipv4.conf.default.log_martians` to `1` enables this feature. Logging these packets allows an administrator to investigate the possibility that an attacker is sending spoofed packets to their system.

audit.en:
Set the following parameters in `/etc/sysctl.conf` or a file in `/etc/sysctl.d/` ending in `.conf`:  

- `net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians = 1`
- `net.ipv4.conf.default.log_martians = 1`

*Example:*  

Run the following script to set the active kernel parameters:  

**Note:** If these settings appear in a canonically later file, or later in the same file, these settings will be overwritten

```
#!/usr/bin/env bash

{
   sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians=1
   sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.default.log_martians=1
   sysctl -w net.ipv4.route.flush=1
}
```

```
# printf '%s\n' "net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians = 1" "net.ipv4.conf.default.log_martians = 1" >> /etc/sysctl.d/60-netipv4_sysctl.conf
```

remediation.en:
Set the following parameters in `/etc/sysctl.conf` or a file in `/etc/sysctl.d/` ending in `.conf`:  

- `net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians = 1`
- `net.ipv4.conf.default.log_martians = 1`

*Example:*  

Run the following script to set the active kernel parameters:  

**Note:** If these settings appear in a canonically later file, or later in the same file, these settings will be overwritten

```
#!/usr/bin/env bash

{
   sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians=1
   sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.default.log_martians=1
   sysctl -w net.ipv4.route.flush=1
}
```

```
# printf '%s\n' "net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians = 1" "net.ipv4.conf.default.log_martians = 1" >> /etc/sysctl.d/60-netipv4_sysctl.conf
```
